catalogue.definition:
This Data Item allows for a more convenient handling of the
messages at the receiver side by further defining the type of
information.
catalogue.remark:
NOTES:
  1. In applications where data of various types is exchanged, the Message
     Type Data Item facilitates the proper message handling at the receiver
     side.
  2. All Message Type values are reserved for common standard use.
catalogue.title: Message Type

catalogue.definition: Identification of the system from which the data is received.
catalogue.remark:
Note:
    - The up-to-date list of SACs is published on the
      EUROCONTROL Web Site (http://www.eurocontrol.int/asterix).
catalogue.title: Data Source Identifier

catalogue.definition: Absolute time stamping expressed as UTC.
catalogue.remark:
Note:

    The time of day value is reset to zero each day at midnight.
catalogue.title: Time of Day

catalogue.definition: Information concerning the configuration and status of a System.
catalogue.remark:
Note:

    A time source is considered as valid when either externally synchronised
    or running on a local oscillator within the required accuracy of UTC.

items.name: NOGO
items.title: Operational Release Status of the System

catalogue.title: System Status

catalogue.definition: Information concerning the configuration and status of the Tracking processors.
catalogue.remark:
Note:

    Both Bits of one TP set to zero means, that this TP is not used
    in the system.
catalogue.title: Tracking Processor Detailed Status

catalogue.definition: Information concerning the configuration and status of the Remote Sensors (RS)
catalogue.title: Remote Sensor Detailed Status

catalogue.definition: Information concerning the configuration and status of the Reference Transponder.
catalogue.title: Reference Transponder Detailed Status

catalogue.definition: Position of the MLT reference point in WGS-84 Coordinates.
catalogue.title: Position of the MLT System Reference Point

catalogue.definition:
Height of the MLT system reference point in two’s complement form.
The height shall use mean sea level as the zero reference level.
catalogue.title: Height of the MLT System Reference Point

catalogue.definition:
WGS-84 undulation value of the MLT system reference point, in meters.
Geoid undulation value is the difference between the ellipsoidal height
and the height above mean sea level
catalogue.title: WGS-84 Undulation
